checkpoint_context.active_numbering_note: Later-wave bounded Ζ remains premature until reconciliation, prerequisite refresh, and gate refresh run against the completed first-wave doctrine packet.

later_wave_category_identifiers.description: Families that would move or rebind authoritative state and therefore depend on deterministic replication and lawful partition-transfer proof.
Families that would require realized distributed replay and proof-anchor continuity, not merely first-wave verification doctrine.
later_wave_category_identifiers.category_note: No standalone later-wave family is named here yet because widening first-wave replay verification into realization would violate the first-wave freeze.

later_wave_category_identifiers.description: Families that touch trust convergence, refusal/remediation posture, revocation, or publication execution.
Families that depend on stronger live-cutover receipt and provenance anchorization.
Future-only movement families that remain outside bounded near-term Ζ.

boundary_class_identifiers.meaning: Plausible later-wave candidate whose bounded posture may be re-analyzed in Ζ-B1.
Family is doctrinally meaningful but still too central to unresolved realization blockers.
Family stays outside bounded near-term Ζ-B planning entirely.
Family is not honest later-wave bounded scope yet even though governing doctrine exists.

candidate_family_entries.title: Trust-aware refusal and containment
why_candidate_or_deferred: The family is narrower than live trust execution and can remain bounded to refusal, containment, and remediation posture.
Ζ-P2 already kept the family review-gated and later-wave-only rather than blocked or future-only.
missing_realization_notes: Distributed trust and authority convergence remains unrealized.
Trust roots remain empty in the live trust-root registry.
Trust policy posture remains provisional rather than gate-passing.
first_wave_freeze_constraints: Must not reinterpret Ζ-A0 staged validation as live trust execution readiness.
Must not reinterpret Ζ-A1 proof-only verification as distributed trust convergence.

candidate_family_entries.title: Live cutover receipt pipeline and anchorization
why_candidate_or_deferred: First-wave doctrine sharpened receipt obligations, cutover evidence posture, and proof-bearing boundaries.
Later-wave refresh may now assess bounded shadow or parallel anchorization without assuming the pipeline is already realized.
missing_realization_notes: Canonical live receipt-pipeline machinery does not yet exist.
Lawful runtime cutover proof remains unresolved across hotswap and distributed-authority boundaries.
Local traces, logs, and CI outputs remain derived evidence rather than canonical receipts.
first_wave_freeze_constraints: Must not reinterpret Ζ-A2 rehearsal records as canonical live-cutover receipts.
Must not use first-wave proof packets to claim receipt-pipeline realization.

candidate_family_entries.title: Bounded authority handoff and state transfer
why_candidate_or_deferred: The family is now doctrinally meaningful because distributed-authority and cutover boundaries are explicit.
The family remains too central to unresolved replication, continuity, cutover, trust, and receipt blockers for honest matrix-ready admission.
missing_realization_notes: Deterministic replication and lawful state-partition transfer proof remain unrealized.
Distributed replay and proof-anchor continuity remain verification-only rather than realized continuity.
Lawful handoff still lacks cutover proof, trust convergence, and stronger receipt posture.
first_wave_freeze_constraints: Must not reinterpret Ζ-A1 verification doctrine as transfer legality.
Must not reinterpret Ζ-A2 rehearsal doctrine as lawful authority movement.
Must not widen Ζ-A0 staged validation into live handoff semantics.

candidate_family_entries.title: Live trust, revocation, and publication execution
why_candidate_or_deferred: This is a realization-heavy family rather than an honest bounded later-wave candidate.
First-wave doctrine did not reduce the live trust, revocation, or publication execution blocker stack.
missing_realization_notes: Live revocation propagation and live publication execution machinery remain unrealized.
first_wave_freeze_constraints: Must not infer live trust execution readiness from refusal-aware first-wave doctrine.
Must not infer publication execution readiness from receipt-generalization doctrine.
downstream_prompt_mapping: later broader Ζ planning only after a future checkpoint materially narrows trust and receipt blockers

candidate_family_entries.title: Live shard relocation
why_candidate_or_deferred: The post-first-wave checkpoint explicitly kept relocation future-only.
The family depends on nearly the entire unresolved realization stack and cannot honestly enter the later-wave refresh band.
missing_realization_notes: Replication proof, continuity realization, cutover proof, trust convergence, receipt-pipeline realization, and trust/publication execution realization all remain unresolved.
Transport or cluster substrate does not narrow those blockers by itself.
first_wave_freeze_constraints: Must not shrink-wrap relocation into first-wave proof-only families.
Must not treat transport success or shard substrate as lawful relocation readiness.
downstream_prompt_mapping: broader Ζ only after future blocker reduction and checkpoint approval

first_wave_freeze_rules: zeta.family.bounded_runtime_cutover_proof_rehearsal remains proof-only and frozen pending unresolved blockers.
No later-wave family may consume first-wave doctrine as realized distributed continuity, lawful authority handoff, lawful live cutover, or canonical live receipt-pipeline realization.

doctrine_vs_realization_markers.meaning: A family can be doctrinally explicit and still remain out of scope or future-only.
Later-wave candidacy only authorizes matrix refresh consideration, not execution or gate passage.
Runtime, trust, shard, and provenance substrate remain evidence roots rather than proof of later-wave readiness.
Completing Ζ-A0..Ζ-A2 does not itself reduce the later-wave blocker stack enough to admit execution.

canonical_vs_derived_distinctions: docs/planning/LATER_WAVE_BOUNDARY_RECONCILIATION.md and this registry are canonical for later-wave boundary truth.
Checkpoint summaries, dashboards, local family maps, and packaging reports are derived and must not redefine family status.
Server, shard, trust, provenance, and registry surfaces are evidence only unless a stronger planning or doctrine source explicitly promotes them.
prohibited_later_wave_reconciliation_sha: widening first-wave families by renaming them later-wave
promoting perimeter families because code surfaces exist
treating realization-heavy items as matrix-ready without reconciliation
inventing new later-wave family ids from substrate names alone
treating candidate status as execution admission

stability_notes.replacement_target: later Ζ-B refresh artifacts and the later-wave admission checkpoint may refine family readiness without replacing the later-wave boundary law frozen here
